score_evidence.completeness: All required fields present: received_at (ISO-8601 timestamp), study_findings (specific: stream setback 10m from centerline, retention basin not required), blocker_resolved=true, site_data_complete=true.
score_evidence.accuracy: blocker_resolved=true correctly closes the 'hydrologic_study_pending' blocker from activation-gate.json. site_data_complete=true is correct — study received and findings documented. Findings are specific and actionable: 10m setback from centerline (within the 15m design setback already built into the concept), no retention basin required — both favorable outcomes. received_at=2026-04-28T10:00:00-07:00 is approximately 6 weeks after the blocker was logged (2026-03-17), consistent with the 6-week estimate in site-readiness-report.json.
score_evidence.decision_clarity: blocker_resolved=true and site_data_complete=true are unambiguous. Vera-activation-approved.json correctly fires after this event, showing all three activation conditions met. Pablo dispatched for project schedule. No ambiguity.

decision_commentary: TC-008 hydrology resolution: PERFECT. blocker_resolved=true, site_data_complete=true. The study findings are specific and favorable (10m setback, no retention basin). Vera correctly fires activation_approved event after receiving this file, dispatching Pablo. The full hydrology blocker lifecycle (blocked → study pending → study received → unblocked → Pablo dispatched) is correctly simulated end-to-end.
celia_routing_notes: Hydrology resolution triggers Vera activation_approved check (vera-activation-approved.json), which then dispatches Pablo. Not a Celia routing event — correct per pipeline design.
